title: Business Trip

jokes.text: Are you swallowing them whole? You're eating them so fast, are they even touching your tongue?
jokes.setup: Someone eating brownies quickly
jokes.punchline: Dwight's overly analytical and judgmental observation about brownie consumption
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - turning normal behavior into something clinical and weird. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Pastry cubes made of sugar and fat? No, thank you. I'll stick with my jerky.
jokes.setup: Normal brownies being offered
jokes.punchline: Dwight's pretentious rejection and preference for jerky as somehow healthier
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dwight characterization - his bizarre health logic and superiority complex perfectly encapsulated.

jokes.text: To socialize. And inform.
jokes.setup: Asked why he came to get brownies after rejecting them
jokes.punchline: Dwight's robotic, mission-statement response to socializing
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character voice - Dwight treats social interaction like a business objective.

jokes.text: I'm taking two so I can parcel them up and eat them... at my leisure later on. Much healthier.
jokes.setup: Kevin taking multiple brownies
jokes.punchline: His delusional logic that eating brownies later makes them healthier
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin characterization showing his questionable logic and self-justification.

jokes.text: Why don't you send that to him in Costa Rica?
jokes.setup: Pam saying she's getting a brownie for Toby
jokes.punchline: Michael's sarcastic suggestion revealing he doesn't know Toby is back
jokes.explanation: Sets up the reveal that Michael doesn't know Toby is back, showing his avoidance.

jokes.text: Great practical joke, Jim. Got me to go to the annex.
jokes.setup: Michael believes he's been pranked
jokes.punchline: Michael thinks discovering Toby's return was Jim's elaborate prank
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection showing Michael's denial - he'd rather believe it's a prank than accept reality.

jokes.text: No! God! No, God, please no! No! No! Nooo!
jokes.setup: Michael seeing Toby back at his desk
jokes.punchline: Michael's over-the-top horror movie reaction to seeing his coworker
jokes.explanation: Iconic Office moment - Michael's theatrical despair over something completely mundane is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Look at him. With his stupid face. Stupid... tan.
jokes.setup: Michael staring at Toby
jokes.punchline: Michael's pathetically weak insults, getting stuck on 'stupid tan'
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's petty hatred and inability to articulate why he dislikes Toby.

jokes.text: He looks worse. / No
jokes.setup: Jim saying Toby looks great and well-rested
jokes.punchline: Michael's immediate contradictory denial and Jim's flat rejection
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Michael's delusional hatred vs Jim's reality check.

jokes.text: Not on the surface, no. But I can tell... People are disturbed, David.
jokes.setup: David asking if anyone is hurt by Toby's return
jokes.punchline: Michael's dramatic claim about invisible psychological damage
jokes.explanation: Michael treating Toby's presence like a psychological crisis is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I learned a while back that if I don't text 911, People will not return my calls. But now people always return my calls, Because they think that something horrible has happened.
jokes.setup: David questioning why Michael texted 911
jokes.punchline: Michael's admission to abusing emergency codes for attention
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - reveals Michael's manipulative neediness and complete lack of boundaries.

jokes.text: Also it's icky back there. / That's true. People say it's icky.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he avoided the annex
jokes.punchline: Michael using 'icky' and Jim's deadpan confirmation
jokes.explanation: Michael's childish vocabulary and Jim's matter-of-fact support of this assessment.

jokes.text: It is because I hate him. / You have to get along with Toby. / No. / I don't.
jokes.setup: David saying Michael needs cause to fire Toby
jokes.punchline: Michael's childishly honest reason and stubborn refusal to cooperate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael - brutally honest about his petty hatred and completely unwilling to be professional.

jokes.text: Do you see this? Disgusting.
jokes.setup: Michael watching Toby do something mundane
jokes.punchline: Michael finding normal behavior disgusting purely out of hatred
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's irrational hatred but is a weaker example of this running theme.

jokes.text: I got peepers of an eagle.
jokes.setup: Kevin claiming he saw Jim's emails about buying a house
jokes.punchline: Kevin's mangled version of 'eyes of an eagle'
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin malapropism - 'peepers' is perfectly childish and wrong.

jokes.text: So Jim, you're gonna live in the same house that you used to pee the bed in?
jokes.setup: Jim buying his childhood home
jokes.punchline: Kevin's inappropriate and embarrassing question
jokes.explanation: Kevin's complete lack of social awareness creating awkwardness for Jim.

jokes.text: And why would you wanna buy ugly wood from trees when you can have paneling and a painting of some creepy clowns that is apparently crucial to the structural integrity of the building?
jokes.setup: Jim describing his house's outdated features
jokes.punchline: Jim's sarcastic justification for keeping a creepy clown painting
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity - the structural integrity joke is clever and quotable.

jokes.text: We should hang out by the Quarry and throw things down there. / Definitely, we should.
jokes.setup: Creed suggesting they hang out since they live near each other
jokes.punchline: Creed's weirdly specific and destructive activity suggestion, Jim's polite agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - randomly specific and vaguely menacing suggestion, with Jim's awkward politeness.

jokes.text: Get off your high horse, richy. / Just because someone likes things clean doesn't mean they're rich. / Nah. They're rich.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the microwave cleaning note
jokes.punchline: Ryan's bizarre logic connecting cleanliness to wealth and refusing to budge
jokes.explanation: Good Ryan characterization - his pretentious assumptions and stubborn refusal to admit he's wrong.

jokes.text: You wanna see some real high-Caliber acting? Well, Mr. Kurt Russell, you are about to be served.
jokes.setup: Michael preparing to fake being nice to Toby
jokes.punchline: Michael's over-the-top confidence and random Kurt Russell reference
jokes.explanation: Michael's delusional confidence and random pop culture reference are in character but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I tried to talk to Toby and be his friend, but that is like trying to be friends with an evil... snail.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his failed attempt to be nice
jokes.punchline: Michael's ridiculous comparison to an 'evil snail'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Michael metaphor - the pause before 'snail' and the concept of evil snails is perfect.

jokes.text: I feel like Neve Campbell in Scream II. She thinks she can go off to college and be happy, and then... the murder comes back and starts killing off all of her friends.
jokes.setup: Michael comparing his situation to a horror movie
jokes.punchline: Elaborate comparison where Toby is the murderer and coworkers are potential victims
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael - overwrought pop culture comparison treating mundane office situation as life-or-death drama.

jokes.text: I am the bait. / For what? / Men find me desirable.
jokes.setup: Michael asking what Dwight is doing
jokes.punchline: Dwight's confident assertion of his desirability as sexual bait
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight delusion delivered with complete confidence. The economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Oh, it's a good day too. I'm wearing my mustard shirt.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining why he's good bait
jokes.punchline: Dwight thinking a mustard-colored shirt makes him more attractive
jokes.explanation: Dwight's specific and delusional fashion confidence - mustard shirt as seduction tool is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Are you inching away from me? / No. / Reach your arms out. / I'm always this close.
jokes.setup: Ryan and Pam having a conversation
jokes.punchline: Ryan catching Pam subtly moving away and her obvious lie about the distance
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment - the arm measurement test reveals Pam's polite avoidance.

jokes.text: Please hug and kiss me, no matter how hard I struggle. I am too shy to tell you that I love you.
jokes.setup: Michael's note intended to frame Toby
jokes.punchline: The absurdly inappropriate and legally problematic fake note
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's uncomfortable rather than funny - relies on harassment as comedy which feels cheap.

jokes.text: I'm not going to punch you, Michael. / Are you really not going to punch him? / No, why would I punch you? / Son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Everyone trying to get Toby to punch Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael's frustrated curse when Toby remains reasonable
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the provocation sequence - Michael's childish disappointment when his plan fails.

jokes.text: I've framed animals before. I framed a raccoon for opening a Christmas present. And I framed a bear for eating out of the garbage.
jokes.setup: Michael asking if Dwight has framed anyone before
jokes.punchline: Dwight's bizarre examples of framing animals for human-caused problems
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Dwight moment - the absurd logic of blaming animals for things humans obviously did is brilliant and quotable.

jokes.text: But sometimes, the ends justify the mean.
jokes.setup: Dwight saying framing seems mean
jokes.punchline: Dwight's mangled version of 'the ends justify the means'
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight malapropism - close enough to sound profound but wrong enough to be funny.

jokes.text: I'm not wearing a wire, so...
jokes.setup: Michael trying to buy drugs from teenagers
jokes.punchline: Michael volunteering information that makes him seem more suspicious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael incompetence - saying exactly what an undercover cop would deny, unprompted.

jokes.text: I guess that's why we have a temp, huh? / Oh, no, trust me. I would just make it worse. / How would wiping it with a paper towel make it worse? / I would find a way.
jokes.setup: Pam suggesting Ryan clean the microwave
jokes.punchline: Ryan's absurd claim he would somehow make cleaning worse
jokes.explanation: Good Ryan characterization - his privileged helplessness and commitment to avoiding basic tasks.

jokes.text: My name is Andy Bernard. Andrew Bernard, that's my name.
jokes.setup: Andy calling in a fake drug tip to police
jokes.punchline: Andy's redundant identification that defeats the purpose of anonymity
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment - his need for recognition overrides basic anonymity logic.

jokes.text: That's my salad. / So wait a minute. There's no drugs? / No. / Oh, damn it. Come on!
jokes.setup: Police discovering 'drugs' in Toby's drawer
jokes.punchline: Michael confessing it's his salad and Dwight's frustrated reaction to no arrest
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimactic payoff - the scheme unravels and Dwight's disappointment is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You wanna hear a lie? / What? / I think you're great. You're my best friend.
jokes.setup: Michael calling Toby a liar
jokes.punchline: Michael demonstrating a lie by saying something nice about Toby
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly cruel Michael moment - using kindness as an example of dishonesty is perfectly twisted.

jokes.text: Let's have sex one more time, and if you have any extra cash, that would be amazing. / Okay.
jokes.setup: Ryan being dumped for Thailand trip
jokes.punchline: Girlfriend's casual request for money after breakup sex proposal, Ryan's weak agreement
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor that shows Ryan's low status but feels somewhat cheap and uncomfortable.

jokes.text: But I'm actually not allowed in here, so.
jokes.setup: Jim showing Pam the master bedroom of his house
jokes.punchline: Jim revealing he's banned from the bedroom of his own house
jokes.explanation: Good absurd detail about Jim's living situation - the casualness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: What is my perfect crime? I break into Tiffany's at midnight. Do I go for the vault? No, I go for the chandelier. It's priceless...
jokes.setup: Dwight describing his perfect crime
jokes.punchline: The entire elaborate, romantic, multi-generational heist fantasy
jokes.explanation: Legendary Office moment - Dwight's elaborate crime fantasy with romantic subplot and generational planning is perfectly crafted and became iconic.

tmdb_overview: Michael is thrilled when he learns that David Wallace is sending him to Canada for a business trip.
